dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.operationalDescription: All systems configured and ready. Your organization can now issue and verify credentials.

guidedSetupBanner.resumeProgress: You're {{completed}} of {{total}} steps complete. Continue the guided setup to finish configuring your organization.

organizationHealth.setupWarning: ⚠️ Complete setup by configuring all required resources to make this organization operational.

developerQuickStart.title: Developer Quick Start
developerQuickStart.description: Org-scoped integration details for API development

issuance.keysReason: Valid signing keys are required to sign and issue verifiable credentials. Without them, credential issuance cannot proceed.
issuance.issuerReason: The issuer service must be active and properly configured to handle credential issuance requests.

verification.deploymentsReason: At least one deployment profile must be active to verify credentials. Deployments define the trust policies and verification rules.
verification.policyReason: Presentation policies must be accessible to validate credentials during verification.

keys.reason: Signing keys authenticate your organization as a credential issuer. expired or invalid keys prevent credential issuance and may invalidate issued credentials.

recentActivity.title: Recent Activity
recentActivity.noActivity: Activity will appear here once you begin issuing or verifying credentials

issuance.title: Credential Issuance
issuance.description: Real-time metrics for OID4VCI credential offers (Last 24 hours)

guidedSetup.title: Guided Organization Setup
guidedSetup.welcomeMessage: Welcome! This wizard will help you set up the essential components for your organization. You can use quick forms here or click "Advanced" for full configuration.
guidedSetup.progressSavedDescription: You can exit and resume anytime.
guidedSetup.setupCompleteMessage: You've configured all the essential components. Your organization is now ready to issue and verify credentials.
guidedSetup.exitConfirm: Are you sure you want to exit the setup wizard? Your progress will be saved.
guidedSetup.setupCompletedNotification: Setup completed! Your organization is ready to use.

lanesDevicesPage.description: Monitor and manage verification lanes and connected devices.

signingKeys.title: Signing Keys
signingKeys.description: Manage cryptographic signing keys for credential issuance and verification

emptyState.title: No signing keys configured
emptyState.description: Signing keys are required to issue and verify credentials. Upload your first key to enable credential issuance.
emptyState.whyItMatters: Without valid signing keys, you cannot issue credentials or verify signatures.

signingKeys.invalidKeysWarning: You have invalid or expired signing keys. Credential issuance may be blocked until you rotate or upload valid keys.

uploadDialog.title: Upload Signing Key
uploadDialog.infoMessage: Store your private key securely. Only the public key is uploaded to the platform.

rotateDialog.title: Rotate Signing Key
rotateDialog.description: Rotating a signing key will generate a new key and mark the current key as deprecated. The old key will remain valid for a grace period to avoid breaking existing credentials.

settingsDialog.title: Key Management Settings
settingsDialog.hsmConfigMessage: Configure your Hardware Security Module connection settings here.
settingsDialog.hsmConfigPlaceholder: HSM configuration options will be displayed here based on your HSM provider.
settingsDialog.vaultConfigMessage: Configure your HashiCorp Vault connection settings here.
settingsDialog.vaultConfigPlaceholder: Vault configuration options will be displayed here.

signingKeys.deleteConfirmation: Are you sure you want to delete this signing key? This action cannot be undone.

empty.whyItMatters: Audit logs help you monitor security, troubleshoot issues, and maintain compliance.
empty.noMatching: No audit events found matching your criteria.

messages.exportJobCreated: Export job created. You'll receive a notification when ready.
messages.enterFilterViewName: Enter a name for this filter view:

sections.emailCategoriesDescription: Choose which types of events should trigger email notifications.

pushOnly.title: Push Notifications Only
pushOnly.description: Receive notifications in the Marty Authenticator app

applicationUpdates.title: Application Updates

trustProfile.title: Build Trust Profile
trustProfile.description: Define trusted issuers and validation rules for credential verification

basicsStep.title: Basic Information
basicsStep.description: Trust Profiles define which credential issuers your organization trusts and what validation rules apply. They're the foundation for secure credential verification across your organization.

helpers.name: You can change this later
helpers.description: Optional: Explain the purpose and scope of this trust profile
helpers.frameworkType: Select the trust framework or standard this profile implements
helpers.supportedFormats: Select which credential formats this trust profile accepts

formatOptions.jwt_vc: JWT VC (JSON Web Token Verifiable Credential)

trustSourcesStep.title: Trust Sources
trustSourcesStep.description: Define which credential issuers your organization trusts. You can skip this step and configure trust sources later.

infoAlert.body: Trust sources can include DIDs (Decentralized Identifiers), certificate authorities, or known issuer endpoints.
infoAlert.skippingDescription: Your profile will accept credentials from any issuer. You can add specific trust sources later to restrict which issuers are accepted.

issuerDid.label: Issuer DID
issuerDid.helper: Enter a DID (Decentralized Identifier) for a trusted issuer

trustSourcesStep.emptyState: No trusted issuers configured yet. Add DIDs above or skip this step.

comingSoon.title: Coming soon:
comingSoon.description: Import trusted issuers from known registries (ICAO PKD, EU Trust Lists, etc.)

validationRulesStep.title: Validation Rules
validationRulesStep.description: Configure cryptographic and security requirements. Defaults are pre-selected based on current best practices.

defaultsAlert.title: Defaults are production-safe.
defaultsAlert.description: These rules determine which credentials are accepted during verification. You can skip this step to use secure defaults.

allowedAlgorithms.title: Allowed Signing Algorithms
allowedAlgorithms.helper: Select which cryptographic algorithms are accepted for credential signatures

keySize.label: Minimum Key Size (RSA)
keySize.helper: Minimum RSA key size for signature verification (applies to RS*/PS* algorithms)

allowSelfSigned.label: Allow self-signed credentials
allowSelfSigned.helper: Enable if credentials can be signed by their subject (not recommended for production)

requireKeyUsage.label: Require proper key usage in certificates
requireKeyUsage.helper: Verify that signing keys have appropriate X.509 key usage extensions (recommended)

reviewStep.title: Review & Activate
reviewStep.description: Review all configuration details before creating the trust profile.

trustSourcesSummary.noneConfigured: No trust sources configured (can be added later)

activationExplanation.title: What happens after creation?
activationExplanation.description: Active trust profiles are immediately available for verification workflows. You'll be redirected to create a credential template that uses this profile.

activateImmediately.label: Activate immediately
activateImmediately.description: Make this trust profile active and ready to use upon creation

success.title: 🎉 Trust Profile Created!
success.messageActive: "{{name}}" is now active and ready to use.
success.nextStep: Next, create a credential template to define what credentials this profile will verify.

credentialTemplate.title: Build Credential Template
credentialTemplate.description: Define what credentials are issued and their structure

basicsStep.title: Basic Information
basicsStep.description: Provide core details about this credential template.

helpers.templateName: A descriptive name for this credential template (e.g., 'Driver License', 'Diploma')
helpers.credentialType: The type of credential this template represents
helpers.vct: A unique URI identifying this credential type (often a URL or URN)
helpers.description: Optional: Describe the purpose and usage of this credential template

claimsStep.title: Define Claims
claimsStep.description: Specify what information (claims) this credential contains.
claimsStep.info: Claims are the individual pieces of information in the credential (e.g., "family_name", "birth_date", "license_number").

quickStart.title: Quick Start Templates
quickStart.description: Choose a preset to auto-populate common claim structures

claimsStep.emptyState: No claims defined yet. Add claims above to define the credential structure.

trustComplianceStep.title: Trust & Compliance
trustComplianceStep.description: Select which trust profile and compliance rules apply to this credential template.

blocked.title: Trust Profile Required
blocked.description: Before creating a credential template, you need at least one active Trust Profile. Trust Profiles define which issuers are trusted and validation rules for credentials.
blocked.alert: You cannot proceed with credential template creation until an active Trust Profile exists. Create one now to continue.

trustProfile.label: Trust Profile
trustProfile.helper: The trust profile that defines validation rules for this credential ({{count}} active profile available)
trustProfile.helper_plural: The trust profile that defines validation rules for this credential ({{count}} active profiles available)

complianceProfile.label: Compliance Profile
complianceProfile.helper: Optional: Select only if you need specific regulatory requirements (GDPR, HIPAA, etc.)

guidance.title: Most users don't need compliance profiles.
guidance.description: Compliance profiles add extra validation layers for regulated industries. Leave this as "None" unless you have specific regulatory requirements.
guidance.securityDescription: The selected Trust Profile will determine which cryptographic algorithms and validation rules apply to credentials issued from this template.

cryptoValidityStep.title: Cryptography & Validity (Optional)
cryptoValidityStep.description: Configure signing and validity settings. Defaults are pre-selected based on best practices.

defaults.title: Defaults are secure and ready to use.
defaults.description: Skip this step or use defaults for most use cases. Advanced users can customize crypto and validity settings below.

validity.title: Validity Period
validity.description: Configure how long credentials remain valid after issuance
validity.defaultValidityHelper: How long credentials are valid by default (e.g., 365 days = 1 year)
validity.maxValidityHelper: Maximum allowed validity period (e.g., 730 days = 2 years)
validity.exampleDescription: With a default validity of {{days}} days, a credential issued today will expire on {{date}}.

signingAlgorithm.label: Signing Algorithm
signingAlgorithm.helper: The cryptographic algorithm used to sign credentials from this template

revocationProfile.label: Revocation Profile
revocationProfile.helper: Optional: Select a revocation mechanism for credentials issued from this template

reviewStep.title: Review & Activate
reviewStep.description: Review all configuration details before creating the credential template.

generateArtifacts.label: Generate cryptographic artifacts automatically
generateArtifacts.description: Automatically create signing keys and certificates for this template

activateImmediately.label: Activate immediately
activateImmediately.description: Make this template active and ready for credential issuance

success.title: 🎉 Template Created!
success.nextStepTitle: Next step: Create an Issuance Flow
success.nextStepDescription: Templates are not applicant-facing. To make this credential available to applicants, create an Issuance Flow that uses this template.

presentationPolicy.title: Build Presentation Policy
presentationPolicy.description: Define verification requirements for credential presentations

blocked.title: Trust Profile Required
blocked.description: Before creating a presentation policy, you need to configure at least one Trust Profile. Trust Profiles define which credential issuers your organization trusts and the validation rules for verifying credentials.
blocked.info: Trust Profiles determine which standards (ICAO, AAMVA, EUDI, etc.) and certificate authorities are trusted for credential verification.

trustProfileStep.title: Select Trust Profile
trustProfileStep.description: Choose which Trust Profile this presentation policy will use for credential validation. The Trust Profile determines which issuers are trusted and what validation rules apply.

customTemplate.name: Custom Policy
customTemplate.description: Build a custom verification policy from scratch

templateSelectionStep.prerequisite: Please select a Trust Profile first.
templateSelectionStep.title: Choose a Template
templateSelectionStep.description: Start with a standards-based template for common verification scenarios, or create a custom policy.
templateSelectionStep.noTemplates: No pre-built templates available for {{framework}} framework. You can still create a custom policy.

selectedInfo.next: You can customize the claims and settings in the next steps.

templateSelectionStep.customInfo: You'll configure all policy settings manually in the next steps.

claimsConfigurationStep.title: Configure Claims
claimsConfigurationStep.description: Define the basic information and required claims for this presentation policy.

placeholders.requiredValue: If specified, the claim must match this exact value

helpers.policyName: A descriptive name for this policy
helpers.purposeStatement: Explain to users why you need this information (shown during consent)
helpers.addCredentialType: Select from templates or enter custom type
helpers.credentialTypesQuestion: Which credential types can satisfy this policy?
helpers.claimName: The name of the claim to request
helpers.fromCredentialType: Which credential type contains this claim
helpers.requiredValue: If specified, the claim must match this exact value

alerts.addCredentialTypeFirst: Add at least one accepted credential type
alerts.addClaim: Add at least one required claim
alerts.addTypesBeforeClaims: Add credential types above before configuring claims
alerts.predicateInfo: Privacy-enhanced: User can prove attributes without revealing actual values (e.g., prove "age over 21" without showing birth date)

captions.preferPredicates: When enabled, predicates are preferred over raw values whenever possible
captions.singlePresentation: All claims must come from a single credential (more restrictive)

freshnessBindingStep.title: Freshness & Security Settings
freshnessBindingStep.description: Configure how recent credentials must be and how to bind presentations to holders.

helpers.holderBinding: How should the presentation be bound to the credential holder?
helpers.freshness: Control how recent credentials and proofs must be.
helpers.maxCredentialAge: How old can the credential be? (1 year = 365 days)
helpers.maxProofAge: How old can the proof be? (typically 5-10 minutes)
helpers.revocationCheck: Verify that credentials have not been revoked (recommended)
helpers.compliance: Track which standard version this policy targets for compliance audits.
helpers.standardReference: Optional: Specify the standard version this policy conforms to

freshnessBindingStep.standardReferenceInfo: This policy will be marked as targeting: {{standard}}

reviewStep.title: Review & Submit
reviewStep.description: Review all configuration details before creating the policy.
reviewStep.editHint: You can edit any section by clicking the "Edit" button on that card.

activation.label: Activate immediately after creation
activation.activeDescription: Policy will be active and available for use in verification requests
activation.inactiveDescription: Policy will be created but remain inactive until manually enabled

success.title: 🎉 Presentation Policy Created!
success.messageActive: "{{name}}" is active and ready to use.
success.nextStep: Next, create a deployment profile to define where and how this policy is enforced.

flowTypeStep.title: Select Flow Type
flowTypeStep.description: Choose the type of flow based on your use case

verification.name: Verification Flow
verification.description: Request and verify credentials from holders (OID4VP)

issuance_oid4vci.name: OID4VCI Issuance (QR Code)
issuance_oid4vci.description: Issue credentials via OID4VCI QR codes for third-party wallets

combined.name: Combined Flow
combined.description: Verify existing credentials, then issue new ones

flowStepsConfigStep.title: Configure Flow Steps
flowStepsConfigStep.description: Define basic information and the sequence of steps for this flow

helpers.flowName: A descriptive name for this flow
helpers.description: Optional details about what this flow accomplishes
helpers.audience: Who will use this flow? (e.g., Applicants, Partners, Internal Staff)

flowStepsConfigStep.emptyState: Add flow steps or use a preset template to get started

preconditionsStep.title: Configure Preconditions
preconditionsStep.descriptionOid4vci: Define when credential offers should be automatically generated. Select one or more conditions that must be met before proceeding.
preconditionsStep.descriptionDefault: Configure preconditions for this flow type.
preconditionsStep.limitedSupport: Precondition configuration is currently optimized for OID4VCI issuance flows. Other flow types may have limited precondition support.
preconditionsStep.summarySingle: The flow will automatically advance when the selected condition is met.
preconditionsStep.summaryMultiple: The flow will automatically advance when ALL selected conditions are met (AND logic).
preconditionsStep.noneSelected: No preconditions selected. The flow will require manual triggering or external orchestration.

application_approved.label: Application Approved
application_approved.description: Automatically trigger when an applicant's credential application is approved

identity_verified.label: Identity Verified
identity_verified.description: Require successful identity verification (biometric, document scan, etc.)

manual_admin_approval.label: Manual Admin Approval
manual_admin_approval.description: Require explicit admin action to proceed with issuance

external_verification.label: External Verification Result
external_verification.description: Wait for external system verification callback (webhook/API)

deploymentBindingStep.title: Bind to Deployment
deploymentBindingStep.description: Optionally bind this flow to a deployment profile and set a default presentation policy. This step can be skipped and configured later.

errors.failedToLoad: Failed to load deployment profiles and policies

deployment.empty: No deployment profiles available. You can create one later and bind it to this flow.
deployment.helper: Select a deployment profile to bind this flow to a specific runtime environment

policy.empty: No presentation policies available. This is only required for verification flows.
policy.helper: Select a default presentation policy for verification steps in this flow
policy.label: Presentation Policy

reviewStep.title: Review & Submit
reviewStep.description: Review all configuration details before creating the flow.

values.noPreconditions: No preconditions configured. The flow will require manual triggering.

activation.label: Activate immediately after creation
activation.activeDescription: Flow will be active and available for use immediately
activation.inactiveDescription: Flow will be created but remain inactive until manually enabled

success.title: 🎉 Flow Created!
success.nextStep: Your flow is ready. You can now deploy it and start accepting applications.

deploymentProfile.title: Build Deployment Profile
deploymentProfile.description: Configure how your identity system integrates with runtime environments

success.title: Deployment Profile Created Successfully!
success.messageActive: "{{name}}" is now active and ready to use.
success.apiKeyGenerated: API key has been generated. Check your deployment profile details.

environmentStep.title: Environment Configuration
environmentStep.description: Define where and how this deployment profile will be used.

helpers.profileName: A descriptive name for this deployment (e.g., 'Airport Gate 5', 'Mobile Verification App')
helpers.description: Optional: Additional context about this deployment
helpers.environmentType: Select the type of environment where this profile will be deployed

api.label: API Service
api.description: REST API for credential issuance and verification

OFFLINE.label: Offline
OFFLINE.description: No network required - uses local validation

blocked.title: Presentation Policy Required
blocked.description: Before creating a deployment profile, you need at least one active Presentation Policy. Presentation Policies define what credentials must be presented during verification.
blocked.alert: You cannot proceed until an active Presentation Policy exists.

runtimeSettingsStep.title: Runtime Settings
runtimeSettingsStep.description: Configure which presentation policy and flows this deployment will use.

helpers.defaultPolicy: The presentation policy used by default in this deployment ({{count}} active policy available)
helpers.defaultPolicy_plural: The presentation policy used by default in this deployment ({{count}} active policies available)
helpers.enabledFlows: Select which types of flows this deployment supports

runtimeSettingsStep.emptyFlows: Select at least one flow type to enable functionality in this deployment.

integrationStep.title: Integration (Optional)
integrationStep.description: Configure webhooks, feature flags, and user experience settings. You can skip this step and configure later.
integrationStep.defaultsInfo: These settings are optional. Skip this step to use defaults.

helpers.webhookUrl: POST requests will be sent to this URL for selected events
helpers.featureFlags: Enable or disable specific features for this deployment
helpers.qrCode: Enable QR code scanning for credential exchange
helpers.nfc: Enable Near Field Communication for contactless exchange
helpers.ux: Customize the look and feel of this deployment

reviewStep.title: Review & Activate
reviewStep.description: Review all configuration details before creating the deployment profile.

generateApiKey.label: Generate API key automatically
generateApiKey.description: Create an API key for programmatic access to this deployment

activateImmediately.label: Activate immediately
activateImmediately.description: Make this deployment profile active and ready to use

settings.title: Organization Settings
settings.description: Manage your organization profile and settings.

membership.title: Email Domain-Based Membership
membership.description: Allow users with specific email domains to automatically discover and join your organization.
membership.discoverableHelp: When enabled, users can find your organization in the directory.
membership.domainsHelp: Users with these email domains can discover your organization.

joinPolicies.auto: Users are automatically added as members
joinPolicies.approval: Join requests must be approved by admin

membership.defaultRoleHelp: Role assigned to users who join via email domain

devices.title: Device Security
devices.description: Configure device registration and push notification settings for credential holders.
devices.requireRegistrationHelp: When enabled, users must register their mobile device before they can receive or use credentials.
devices.pushNotificationsHelp: Enable push notifications for credential updates, verification requests, and other time-sensitive events.

promptHelp.onboarding: Users prompted to register device immediately after account creation
promptHelp.firstAction: Users prompted when they first apply for or use a credential
promptHelp.never: Device registration is optional unless required above

danger.title: Danger Zone
danger.description: Deleting your organization will permanently remove all data including credentials, templates, policies, and team members. This action cannot be undone.

team.title: Team
team.subtitle: Manage team members, roles, and invitations

empty.title: No team members yet
empty.description: Invite team members to collaborate on your organization.

actions.confirmRemove: Remove {{email}} from the team? They will lose access immediately.

invite.title: Invite Team Member
invite.info: An invitation email will be sent to the provided address. The invite will expire in 7 days.

changeRole.title: Change Member Role
changeRole.warning: Changing a member's role will affect their access to resources immediately.

webhooks.title: Webhooks
webhooks.description: Configure webhook endpoints to receive real-time event notifications.
webhooks.empty: No webhooks configured. Add a webhook to receive event notifications.

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.subtitle: Manage notifications, alert rules, and preferences

empty.title: No notifications
empty.descriptionAll: You don't have any notifications yet.

error.markAsRead: Failed to mark notification as read
error.markAllAsRead: Failed to mark all as read

empty.title: No alert rules
empty.description: Create alert rules to get notified about important events.

membership.title: Membership Requests
membership.description: Review and approve membership requests from users wanting to join your organization.

empty.title: No Pending Requests
empty.description: When users request to join your organization, they will appear here for review.

dialog.title: Reject Membership Request
dialog.message: Are you sure you want to reject the membership request from
dialog.reasonPlaceholder: Provide a reason that will be visible to the applicant...

roleEscalation.title: Role Escalation Requests
roleEscalation.subtitle: Review and manage role change requests from organization members

dialog.title: Reject Role Request
dialog.message: You are rejecting the role escalation request from
dialog.reasonPlaceholder: Provide a reason for rejecting this request...

defaultsSection.title: Organization Defaults
defaultsSection.description: Set default resources that will be automatically selected when creating new flows and configurations.
